name: BTC 1h Asymmetric Continuation v1
edge_mechanism: continuation is entered after directional reset, but trigger, risk, and exit roles are intentionally separated so the trade can survive small noise while harvesting asymmetry
thesis: Cycle 74 explicitly wants trend continuation with asymmetric exit logic where trigger, risk, and exit indicators play different roles. BTC 1h is the best fit because it is similar enough to ETH to preserve mechanism but often cleaner in structure. The research variable here is role separation: breakout/reclaim trigger, ATR-based invalidation, and EMA-based exit management must do different jobs.
invalidation_condition: If BTC 1h continuation attempts remain anti-correlated or if asymmetric exits still fail to preserve positive skew, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price resets, reasserts directional control, and continues, while exit logic allows winners more room than losers without using the same indicator for everything.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: A common failure mode in earlier families was indicator role collapse. Separating trigger, risk, and exit functions can preserve edge even when simple entry conditions are only modestly predictive.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 1h is the best fit because it is active enough for dense continuation tests but usually less erratic than AXS 1h.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Continuation may still be too weak in chop, causing the runner to give back the asymmetry the stop was trying to preserve.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is fine but winners decay, shorten the time-based de-risk step before touching the trigger.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A moderate number of continuation attempts, many small losses/wins, and a smaller number of larger asymmetry-carrying trades.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 1h clears density and the separated-role exit structure outperforms one-shot symmetric exits.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-20 trades, or a profile where the runner never compensates for stop-outs.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable on 1h because reset/continuation events occur regularly

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit lane for role-separated continuation testing.
test_lanes.continue_if: BTC 1h shows enough continuation attempts with positive asymmetry from the exit structure.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h continuation is too weak or too noisy for the asymmetry to matter.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate participation with a few larger winners carrying the expectancy.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same role-separated continuation works on the cycle primary asset.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 1h preserves the same trigger/risk/exit separation edge.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 1h proves the concept is too lane-specific.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Slightly noisier but comparable asymmetry profile.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same grammar survives when slowed down, even if count falls.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h shows a lower-count but still coherent continuation pattern.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the concept becomes too sparse off 1h.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Lower count, cleaner continuation carry than 1h.

long: Trail remaining 60% by 0.90 * ATR_14 while close remains >= EMA_55
Time-based de-risk: cut 20% after 6 bars if no 1.2 ATR follow-through
short: Trail remaining 60% by 0.90 * ATR_14 while close remains <= EMA_55
Time-based de-risk: cut 20% after 6 bars if no 1.2 ATR follow-through
